user_query:
I’m organizing a fan-led “Halsey Appreciation Week” and need you to turn my ideas into a usable mini content plan I can share and act on. Please work entirely locally (no internet needed) using the files in this workspace.

Requirements and deliverables:
1) Update the campaign configuration so it reflects this week’s plan:
   - Edit config/campaign.json to set:
     • campaign_name: keep as “Halsey Appreciation Week”
     • start_date and end_date based on the earliest and latest date_range_* in input/content_ideas.csv (inclusive)
     • hashtags to include at least ["#Halsey", "#HalseyAppreciationWeek"]
     • channels to include ["twitter", "instagram", "instagram_reels"]
     • time_slots must include at least two slots for twitter (e.g., afternoon/evening UTC), at least one for instagram, and at least one for instagram_reels. Keep timezone as UTC.
   - These settings must be used by the calendar builder to schedule posts.

2) Generate a deterministic content calendar CSV:
   - If tools/calendar_builder.py skips any items because it doesn’t recognize a channel/format (notably channel "instagram_reels" or format "thread"), modify the code to support them so nothing valid is dropped.
   - Then run the script so it reads config/campaign.json and input/content_ideas.csv and writes out/calendar.csv.
   - Scheduling rules should remain deterministic as implemented in the script: schedule on date_range_start at the first available time slot for that channel; if a date/channel exceeds the max per-day limit from the config, shift forward day-by-day within the item’s date_range until it fits; otherwise omit.
   - The resulting out/calendar.csv must include rows for every idea that fits within its date range after your config/code updates, including any with channel "instagram_reels" and format "thread".

3) Prepare meeting notes with concrete action items:
   - Create out/meeting_notes.md with three sections: Agenda, Decisions, Action Items.
   - In Decisions, summarize the campaign_name, date range, timezone, and the total scheduled posts per channel taken from out/calendar.csv.
   - In Action Items, list one line per scheduled idea_id with: owner, channel, scheduled_date, and a short task description. Use this assignment rule for determinism: owner = "Me" for priority "High"; owner = "Alex" for any other priority.

4) Draft two outreach messages that reference the actual plan:
   - Create out/email_merch.txt as a short email to a local merch shop proposing a cross-promo during the campaign. Start with a "Subject:" line. In the body, reference the campaign_name, the start/end dates from config/campaign.json, and the total number of scheduled posts per channel from out/calendar.csv, and include a clear call-to-action to confirm or share assets.
   - Create out/dm_mods.txt as a friendly DM to fan club moderators with the same references (campaign_name, date range, channel counts) and a call-to-action to approve cross-posting.

Consistency checks that must pass:
- out/calendar.csv dates fall within the campaign start/end and each idea’s date range.
- The meeting notes and both messages must use the campaign_name and dates exactly as in the updated config and the per-channel counts must match what’s in out/calendar.csv.

Input and output paths:
- Inputs: input/content_ideas.csv, config/campaign.json, tools/calendar_builder.py
- Outputs to create/overwrite: out/calendar.csv, out/meeting_notes.md, out/email_merch.txt, out/dm_mods.txt

Please avoid using the internet; everything you need is in the provided files. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
